MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO MTN FOR PARTIAL JUDGMNT FILED BY PLAINTIFF TINA SOBERANIS, EXECUTOR OF THE ESTATE OF INGRID ARMBRUST

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO OTHER APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS KATHERINE GRIMME AND JOE CASTELLANO'S (AMENDED) MOTION FOR PARTIAL JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 FOR PARTITION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS CONCEDE THAT PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO AN INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT FOR PARTITION. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS GRIMME AND CASTELLANO ARE TENANTS IN COMMON AND PLAINTIFF OWNS A 50% UNDIVIDED INTEREST IN 4131-4133 CLEMENT ST. AND DEFENDANTS OWN THE REMAINING 50%. THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE APPOINTMENT OF A REFEREE TO EFFECTUATE THE SALE. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A REFEREE AND SALE PROCESS, THEN EITHER SIDE MAY FILE ANOTHER MOTION. THE COSTS OF THE PARTITION WILL COME FROM THE SALE PROCEEDS. THE APPORTIONMENT OF THE PARTITION COSTS WILL BE DETERMINED AFTER THE SALE WITH THE INPUT OF THE REFEREE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT WILL BE HEARD ON OCTOBER 24, 2012 AT 9:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 501, THE HOUSING COURT. IN COMPLIANCE WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6C, MOVING PARTY SHALL PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 501 NO LATER THAN 4PM ON SEPTEMBER 28. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-24-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 10TH AND 11TH. TENTH (10TH) BREACH OF CONTRACT FAILED TO SET FORTH ANY TERMS RE BREACH. ELEVENTH (11TH) FAILS TO SPECIFY PART OF CC 2924 BREACHED AND HOW BREACHED. SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION TWELVE (12) CC 2923.5. DEED OF TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 2008 I.E., 2923.5 NOT APPLICABLE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. SMITH, NOT REPORTED

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cause Of Action 2 For Partition
Off Calendar May-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Denise A. Leadbetter, Esq., of Law Offices of Denise Leadbetter, Tel. 415.713.8680, appearing for Defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KATHERINE GRIMME and JOE CASTELLANO'S (Amended) Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cause Of Action 2 For Partition is Granted. Defendants concede that Plaintiff is entitled to an interlocutory judgment for partition. Plaintiff and Defendants Grimme and Castellano are tenants in common and plaintiff owns a 50% undivided interest in 4131-4133 Clement St. and defendants own the remaining 50%. The parties are to meet and confer regarding the appointment of a referee to effectuate the sale. If the parties cannot agree on a referee and sale process, then either side may file another motion. The costs of the partition will come from the sale proceeds. The apportionment of the partition costs will be determined after the sale with the input of the referee. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew K. Hindman, Esq., from Mastromonaco Real Property Law Group, 415-956-4030 ext, 2, for Plaintiff and opposing party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. DEFENDANTS KATHERINE GRIMME AND JOE CASTELLANO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled with ten days to answer. The complaint clearly alleges that defendants take the position that the August 5 agreement is not valid and the memorandum of agreement and non-partition agreement is at least arguably premised on the August 5 agreement. Therefore, it is permissible alternate pleading for plaintiff to allege that, if the August 5 agreement is invalid as contended by defendants, then there is no contractual bar to partition. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
